candidates.name: CI Babysitter Loop
candidates.summary: Repeated user requests to inspect CI logs before patching and to avoid pushing before verification.
user_semantics: The user repeatedly asks the agent to inspect CI logs before patching.
tool_patterns: CI failure status supports a repeatable observe-verify cycle.
failure_paths: Guessing before reading failed logs causes repeated CI triage churn.
verifier_habits: Run local verification before push or merge.
candidates.why_this_loop: CI triage has observable state, bounded actions, verifier evidence, and approval boundaries.
candidates.why_not_smaller: A rule can remind the agent to read logs, but it cannot preserve CI failure state across attempts.
candidates.why_not_more_autonomous: Push and merge require human approval.
where_this_may_be_wrong: Synthetic packets may omit the current CI provider configuration.

evidence.snippet: CI is red again. Please inspect the failed job logs before guessing.
Same pattern as last time: wait for CI, read the failed log, then patch only the failing test.
Do not push yet. First verify locally and tell me whether the CI failure is actionable.

verification: CI becomes green or is clearly blocked.

managed_loop.objective: Keep CI failures moving toward a verified fix without guessing.
cadence_or_trigger: When CI is pending or failed on the current branch.
cycle_steps: Read the previous state file if it exists.
Inspect CI status, failed logs, and current git diff.
Pick at most 1-3 actionable failures by impact and confidence.
Attempt only low-risk local fixes with direct evidence.
Run focused verification and record the result.

success_criteria: CI becomes green or is clearly blocked.
verifier_commands: Run the focused project checks listed in verification.
completion_contract.evaluator_agent: Use deterministic checks first; use a read-only checker when commands cannot decide.
pass_evidence_required: Command output, CI status, or explicit verifier note.
completion_contract.no_progress_policy: Stop when the same failure repeats twice or no evidence changes across two iterations.

continue_only_if: Next action stays inside approved scope.
A verifier can reject bad output.
New evidence changed or is likely from the next verifier.
Fewer than 3 item(s) are active in this cycle.
done_when: CI becomes green or is clearly blocked.
budget_stopped_when: More than 3 item(s) would be required in one cycle.

status_protocol.CONTINUE: Only when another cycle can increase verified certainty.
status_protocol.DONE: Success criteria passed with required pass evidence; return for acceptance.
status_protocol.NEEDS_HUMAN: Human judgment or explicit approval is required.
status_protocol.BLOCKED: Reliable progress is not possible with current evidence or verifier.
status_protocol.BUDGET_STOPPED: Item, iteration, time, token, or cost cap was reached.

managed_loop.change_policy: If a fix is low risk and directly evidenced, use an isolated branch or worktree when available. Do not push or merge without approval.
deliverables: Patch or branch/PR draft when verification passes
managed_loop.resume_policy: On the next run, read the state file and continue unresolved failures before new ones.
managed_loop.failure_policy: If the same failure repeats twice or verification is inconclusive, record the blocker and stop.
